title: Same-lap pace-shock field context
summary: Capture race-lap-wide pace disruptions by summarizing LapTime_Delta dispersion, positive-share, and changes versus the previous race lap.
rationale: The run explored latent pace-shock blocks; they were not among the strongest families but address a plausible mechanism where safety-car-like or traffic disruptions alter pit timing. This is distinct from individual tyre ageing.
implementation_hint: For each Year-Race-LapNumber, compute median LapTime_Delta, spread, upper-tail share, extreme-slow share, and the difference from the prior race-lap median. Add row-minus-field pace shock and interactions with current pit feasibility.
expected_effect: Small AUC gain with low-medium confidence.
risk: LapTime_Delta has extreme outliers, so robust clipping is required. Same-lap aggregates must not include future laps, and row alignment must be tested carefully.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
